text:
Once upon a time, in a sleepy little town nestled between rolling hills and ancient forests, there lived a girl named Pearl. Pearl loved everything woodland: owls calling nightly from their mossy homes atop oaktrees, foxes whispering secrets amongst cherry blossoms. Saturdays were her favorite day of the week. Her grandma would knit her striped socks,. Auntie Lois would pot herbs.

One sunny afternoon Pearl decided she wanted berries. She scampered through tall grasses across Whispering Willow Creek. There were raspberries ripe as summer moons! She gathered handfuls.

Then came{oatskins}. Rows and rows. She stuffed pockets full. Later that evening, pearl tossed berries everywhere. Next mornings everything smelled funny.

Then Pearl heard strange noises. Low sounds. Owl noises?

She went upstairs quietly. Heard a owl hoot.

That night slept nothing. Ofter owls.

Then pearl dreamed vividly of an owl saying something special.

When Pearl woke,ofter something.

She peeked downstairs.
